2606.30085.title: Not-quite-human tastes: the stylized omnivorousness of LLM survey surrogates
2606.30085.paper_summary: Large-language models have proven to be remarkable if inconsistent parrots of public attitudes and opinions. The extent to which LLMs are able to produce reasonable approximations of cultural taste remains an open empirical question that becomes more urgent by the day, with market research companies already offering provisional `synthetic' survey panels and the contamination of standard survey data from LLM-generated responses. In this study, we build on past work on silicon sampling by extending considerations of its algorithmic fidelity and alignment to the domain of cultural consumption. We use large-language models from OpenAI, Anthropic, and DeepSeek to each produce 277,470 (30x9249) silicon surrogates of survey respondents from the Survey of Public Participation in the Arts (SPPA). We find these silicon surrogates' tastes to be highly stylized facsimiles of human tastes. (1) Silicon samples have a systematic postive-bias for liking, resulting in inflated ecological estimates of tastes. The individual-level bias of silicon samples are not well-explained by the WEIRD-bias often discussed in the literature. (2) The complex relationality in real taste structures is completely lost among silicon samples. (3) Finally, very little of the known cultural alignment between tastes and social space are preserved. Silicon samples attenuate age-taste associations, resurrect anachronistic class-taste associations, caricaturize gender- and race-taste associations.
2606.30085.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型已被證明是公共態度和意見的卓越但不一致的模仿者。大型語言模型能夠產生合理的文化品味近似的程度仍然是一個開放的實證問題，隨著市場研究公司已經提供臨時的「合成」調查小組以及標準調查數據受到大型語言模型生成的回應污染，這個問題變得越來越迫切。在本研究中，我們基於過去在矽樣本方面的工作，擴展了對其算法忠實度和與文化消費領域的對齊考量。我們使用來自OpenAI、Anthropic和DeepSeek的大型語言模型，各自生成277,470（30x9249）個來自公共參與藝術調查（SPPA）的調查受訪者的矽替代品。我們發現這些矽替代品的品味是人類品味的高度風格化的複製品。(1) 矽樣本對於喜好的系統性正偏差，導致品味的生態估計被膨脹。矽樣本的個體層級偏差無法用文獻中經常討論的WEIRD偏差來很好地解釋。(2) 真實品味結構中的複雜關聯性在矽樣本中完全消失。(3) 最後，已知的品味與社會空間之間的文化對齊幾乎沒有被保留。矽樣本削弱了年齡與品味之間的關聯，復活了過時的階級與品味之間的關聯，並對性別和種族與品味之間的關聯進行了漫畫化。
2606.30085.author: Xiangyu Ma et.al.
2606.30085.authors: Xiangyu Ma, Mengmi Zhang, Shannon Ang, Minne Chen
